questions.question: Explain the three capacity planning strategies with their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Lead Strategy: Adds capacity before needed - advantages: prevents downtime, disadvantages: may waste resources
Lag Strategy: Adds capacity after demand exceeds - advantages: no over-provisioning, disadvantages: risk of downtime
Match Strategy: Adds incrementally - advantages: balanced approach, disadvantages: requires frequent adjustments

questions.question: Define and explain the key performance metrics used in capacity planning.
keyPoints: CPU Utilization: Percentage of CPU time in use (target <80% for sustained)
Throughput: Tasks completed per unit time
Response Time: Time from request to first response
Wait Time: Time spent in ready queue
Turnaround Time: Total time from submission to completion

questions.question: Describe the capacity planning lifecycle steps.
keyPoints: 1. Establish baselines - measure normal performance
2. Monitor current performance - continuous tracking
3. Analyze trends and growth - identify patterns
4. Forecast future needs - predict requirements
5. Plan and implement changes - add resources
6. Validate improvements - verify effectiveness

questions.question: Explain the concept of bottleneck in system capacity with an example.
keyPoints: Definition: Component limiting overall system performance due to high utilization
Example: Fast CPU with slow hard disk creates bottleneck at storage
Impact: Determines system throughput regardless of other components
Identification: Resource with utilization >80-90%

questions.question: What is workload characterization? Explain its importance in capacity planning.
keyPoints: Workload characterization: Process of identifying types/quantities of workloads
Types: Batch, interactive, real-time, transactional
Importance: Helps determine resource requirements per workload type
Methods: Profiling, monitoring, classification

questions.question: Explain the role of Operating System in capacity planning.
keyPoints: Resource Management: CPU scheduling, memory allocation, I/O handling
Performance Monitoring: Provides tools to track utilization
Virtualization: Enables flexible resource allocation
Security: Manages access controls affecting performance
Reporting: Generates logs and statistics for analysis

questions.question: What is benchmarking? How is it used in capacity planning?
keyPoints: Definition: Standardized tests to measure performance under controlled conditions
Types: Synthetic benchmarks, application benchmarks, benchmark suites
Usage: Compare systems, identify bottlenecks, predict performance
Examples: SPEC, TPC, LINPACK
